title: Cathay Pacific and Sinopec Join Forces in a Sustainable Aviation Fuel Initiative
content: Cathay Pacific today announced it has entered into an agreement with China Petroleum & Chemical Corporation (Sinopec) to refuel some of its flights departing from Hong Kong International Airport with sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) produced and blended with conventional aviation fuel by Sinopec, demonstrating the expanding reach of SAF produced in the Chinese Mainland in the global SAF supply chain. The airline uplifted a batch of SAF produced by the Sinopec Zhenhai Refining & Chemical Company (ZRCC) at Hong Kong International Airport in April. This batch of SAF was converted from used cooking oil using the hydrotreated esters and fatty acids (HEFA) pathway.
author: Analysts Account
